risinfo.chaptertitle: Appendix II. Test Your Knowledge! Answer Key

listitem.para: a. For minor procedures, separate payment for a significant, separately identifiable E/M service may be allowed when modifier 25 is appended to the E/M code.
d. Codes 15853 and 15854 are add-on codes reported when sutures and/or staples are removed without anesthesia at a follow-up E/M service to a procedure with a 0-day global period.
d. Removal of an umbilical clamp is one of several services that are included in the E/M service and not separately reported.

a. If multiple wounds belong to the same category of difficulty and location, add the lengths and report with a single code. If multiple wounds do not belong in the same category, report each repair separately.

c. Intravenous infusion for hydration, 96360, may be reported for hydration infusion lasting longer than 31 minutes and up to 1 hour. But subsequent code 96361 is reported if an IV hydration infusion is provided secondary or subsequent to a therapeutic, prophylactic, or diagnostic infusion.
